what_it_is: TRIZ contradiction matrix used for General engineering (mechanical, structural, thermal, chemical), Classical TRIZ education and certification in Russian-speaking countries, Historical TRIZ research and scholarship, Patent analysis using original Soviet-era methodology, Cross-referencing with Altshuller Foundation source materials problem framing.

when_to_use.ideal_user_profile: A Russian-speaking engineer, TRIZ practitioner, or researcher who needs the authoritative original parameter terminology for professional work — whether teaching TRIZ at a CIS-region university, publishing in Russian-language innovation journals, consulting for Russian-speaking industrial clients, cross-referencing with Altshuller's original texts and the altshuller.ru archive, or building bilingual TRIZ software. Also suitable for TRIZ historians and scholars studying the evolution of inventive-problem-solving methodology from its Soviet origins.
best_for: Russian-speaking TRIZ practitioners who think and discuss contradictions in their native terminology
Building bilingual (RU/EN) TRIZ software tools with a single authoritative data source
Academic work requiring citation of original Altshuller parameter names
Cross-referencing matrix lookups against Soviet-era TRIZ publications and patent databases
TRIZ certification training programs conducted in Russian
Verifying English translations of TRIZ parameters against the canonical Russian originals
Mechanical, structural, and thermal engineering contradiction resolution in Russian-language contexts
Harmful-factor and reliability analysis — the densest coverage areas of this matrix
not_suitable_for: English-only teams with no need for Russian terminology
Software engineering, business, or management contradiction resolution (no domain-specific parameters)
Problems centered on stationary-object energy use (only 15 cells in that row)
Users who need fully Russian-language principles and sub-principles (only parameters have name_ru)
Modern extended matrices (Matrix 2003/2010) with expanded parameter sets
Information-system or data-centric contradictions (sparse coverage in Loss of Information)
when_to_use.prefer_over_alternatives_when: Use this matrix instead of the English-only classic version when: (1) the user or audience reads/speaks Russian and benefits from seeing original Altshuller terminology; (2) the work product must cite or cross-reference original Soviet TRIZ sources; (3) a bilingual tool needs both EN and RU parameter names from a single file; (4) you are teaching or studying TRIZ history and need the authoritative naming. The underlying cell data is identical to the English classic, so the choice is purely about language context and traceability to the Russian-language TRIZ canon.

sparsest_rows.parameter: Use of energy by stationary object
Duration of action of stationary object

strengths.why: The name_ru fields provide the original parameter names as formulated by Altshuller himself, eliminating translation ambiguity. Terms like 'Устойчивость состава объекта' carry nuances lost in English approximations.

Enables precise cross-referencing with original publications (e.g., 'Алгоритм изобретения', 'Творчество как точная наука') and the altshuller.ru foundation archive without needing a translation mapping.

Densest rows are 'Вредные факторы, действующие на объект' (38 cells), 'Устойчивость состава объекта' (37 cells), and 'Потери вещества' (37 cells) — the matrix is richest for safety, durability, and material-loss contradictions.
Having both name and name_ru side-by-side makes this the ideal data source for bilingual TRIZ software, enabling UI language switching without maintaining separate matrix files.

All 39 parameters and 40 inventive principles are present with 1244 populated cells (83.9% fill rate), identical to the canonical Altshuller matrix. No data is missing relative to the English version.
'Сложность контроля и измерения' (Difficulty of detecting and measuring) has 37 populated cells, making it exceptionally well-covered for instrumentation and metrology contradictions.

weaknesses.why: 'Расход энергии неподвижного объекта' has only 15 cells, 'Продолжительность действия неподвижного объекта' has 20, and 'Объём неподвижного объекта' has 23. Problems centered on static structures will find fewer suggested principles.
'Потери информации' (Loss of Information) has only 22 populated cells — the matrix was built from mechanical/physical patent analysis and offers limited guidance for information-centric problems.

While parameters carry name_ru, the 40 inventive principles themselves are listed only in English. Users seeking a fully Russian-language experience will need to supplement principle names from an external source.
Like all classical Altshuller matrices, this does not cover software, business, or biological domains. The 39 parameters are rooted in 1970s-era physical/mechanical patent analysis.
Sub-principles are English-only, which limits the usefulness for monolingual Russian speakers who need the full detail chain in their native language.
